demos.title: Running /init to Generate CLAUDE.md

key_frames.caption: Claude analyzes codebase and generates CLAUDE.md summary
Permission prompt — Enter to accept or Shift+Tab for auto-accept

walkthrough_steps: Instructor opens terminal and starts Claude Code with `claude` command
Runs `/init` command — Claude analyzes entire codebase
Claude figures out project purpose, architecture, relevant commands, critical files
Summarizes findings and writes them to CLAUDE.md file
Permission prompt appears — can hit Enter to accept or Shift+Tab for free file writes

demos.title: Using # Memory Mode to Add Custom Instructions

key_frames.caption: Typing # to enter memory mode in Claude Code
Adding 'don't write comments so often' instruction

walkthrough_steps: Instructor explains Claude is adding too many comments in code
Types `#` in Claude Code to enter memory mode
Enters instruction: 'don't write comments so often'
Specifies to add instruction to project CLAUDE.md file
Claude intelligently merges instruction into existing CLAUDE.md
Opens file and verifies the instruction was added

demos.title: Using @ File Mentions for Targeted Context

key_frames.caption: Adding schema reference to CLAUDE.md via # memory mode
Claude answers data structure question without reading schema file

walkthrough_steps: Instructor shows how to use `@` to mention files in chat — includes file contents in request
Example: `How does the auth system work? @auth` — Claude shows auth-related files to choose from
Shows using `@` syntax inside CLAUDE.md to permanently reference important files
Uses `#` memory mode to add the schema reference to CLAUDE.md
Asks 'what attributes does a user have?' — Claude answers immediately without reading schema file
File contents from @ mentions are automatically included in every request
